declarations.comments:
A DataLoader batches and caches individual key lookups.
Create one per request to avoid stale caches across requests.
declarations.title: DataLoader

declarations.comments: Create a new DataLoader with a batch function.
declarations.title: mkDataLoader

declarations.comments: Load a single key. Batches with other concurrent loads and caches the result.
declarations.title: load

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL query string with wrappers
declarations.title: queryWith

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL query string against a schema
declarations.title: query

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL request with wrappers applied
declarations.title: executeRequestWith

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL request against a schema, returning a structured response
declarations.title: executeRequest

declarations.comments:
Derives the Om-wrapped implementation row from a pure schema row.
Plain fields get wrapped in Om, function fields get their return type wrapped.
declarations.title: ImplType

declarations.comments: Extracts the row from a Record type
declarations.title: RecordRow

declarations.comments: GraphQL ID scalar — accepts both String and Int inputs
declarations.title: ID

declarations.comments:
Maps a PureScript type to its GraphQL type-level representation and runtime behavior.
The fundep `a -> gql` means the GraphQL schema shape is fully determined by the PureScript type.
declarations.title: GqlSchema

declarations.comments: An EventSource emits values to subscribers.
declarations.title: EventSource

declarations.comments: Subscribe to an EventSource. Returns an unsubscribe effect.
declarations.title: subscribe

declarations.comments: Create a new EventSource.
declarations.title: mkEventSource

declarations.comments: Emit a value to all current subscribers.
declarations.title: emit

declarations.comments:
Upload scalar for GraphQL file uploads.
At the schema level, Upload is a String scalar (filename).
Actual file data is injected by the multipart HTTP handler.
declarations.title: Upload

declarations.comments: A collection of wrappers that can be applied to a schema
declarations.title: Wrapper

declarations.comments: Field wrapper: wraps individual field resolution
declarations.title: FieldWrapper

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: check depth, field count, and cost in a single pass
declarations.title: validateLimits

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: apply a timeout to execution (in milliseconds)
declarations.title: timeout

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: reject queries with more than maxFields total fields
declarations.title: maxFields

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: reject queries deeper than maxDepth
declarations.title: maxDepth

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: reject queries exceeding a cost budget
declarations.title: maxCost

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: log fields that take longer than a threshold (in milliseconds)
declarations.title: logSlowFields

declarations.comments:
"dependency injection" via `ReaderT` which tracks a context, and
checked exceptions and early return with `ExceptV`.
declarations.title: Om

declarations.comments:
Invoke this function in the end and handle all possible errors, e.g.
```purescript
runOm { token: "123" } { someError: \e -> handleTheError e } someApp
```
declarations.title: runOm

declarations.comments:
Run multiple `Om`s in parallel, and collect all results
To only take the fastest result, use `race`
declarations.title: inParallel

contents.contents: Unexpected '}' — not inside a nested selection
Expected '{' after type name in fragment spread
Expected '{' after type name in fragment spread
Expected '... on Type { fields }' inside union selection

declarations.comments:
A DataLoader batches and caches individual key lookups.
Create one per request to avoid stale caches across requests.
declarations.title: DataLoader

declarations.comments: Create a new DataLoader with a batch function.
declarations.title: mkDataLoader

declarations.comments: Load a single key. Batches with other concurrent loads and caches the result.
declarations.title: load

declarations.comments: Decode a GraphQL request from a ResponseValue (parsed JSON)
declarations.title: decodeRequest

declarations.comments: Encode a GraphQL response to a ResponseValue (for JSON serialization)
declarations.title: encodeResponse

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL request against a schema, returning a structured response
declarations.title: executeRequest

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL request with wrappers applied
declarations.title: executeRequestWith

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL query string against a schema
declarations.title: query

declarations.comments: Execute a GraphQL query string with wrappers
declarations.title: queryWith

declarations.comments: Convenience wrapper: no variables, assumes query operation.
declarations.title: parseQuery

declarations.comments: Parse a query with variable values, returning operation type and resolved selections.
declarations.title: parseOperation

declarations.comments: Parse with @defer support, separating deferred fragments from immediate selections.
declarations.title: parseOperationWithDefer

declarations.comments: Extracts the row from a Record type
declarations.title: RecordRow

declarations.comments:
Derives the Om-wrapped implementation row from a pure schema row.
Plain fields get wrapped in Om, function fields get their return type wrapped.
declarations.title: ImplType

declarations.comments:
Walks a schema RowList and partitions fields into query/mutation/subscription
rows based on the Query/Mutation/Subscription wrapper, plus a
combined implementation row.
declarations.title: SplitSchema

declarations.comments: GraphQL ID scalar — accepts both String and Int inputs
declarations.title: ID

declarations.comments:
Maps a PureScript type to its GraphQL type-level representation and runtime behavior.
The fundep `a -> gql` means the GraphQL schema shape is fully determined by the PureScript type.
declarations.title: GqlSchema

declarations.comments: Walks a record's RowList and produces both runtime GqlFields and a type-level row of field types.
declarations.title: GqlSchemaFields

declarations.comments: Produces the runtime argument list for a field type.
declarations.title: GqlFieldArgs

declarations.comments: Maps a PureScript argument type to its GraphQL input type.
declarations.title: GqlArgTypes

declarations.comments: Maps argument record RowList to type-level argument row.
declarations.title: ToGqlArgs

declarations.comments: Determines whether a field is plain or has arguments, producing the appropriate GqlField/GqlFieldWithArgs.
declarations.title: GqlFieldType

declarations.comments: Dispatch GqlSchema behavior based on the GqlKind tag
declarations.title: GqlSchemaByKind

declarations.comments: An EventSource emits values to subscribers.
declarations.title: EventSource

declarations.comments: Create a new EventSource.
declarations.title: mkEventSource

declarations.comments: Emit a value to all current subscribers.
declarations.title: emit

declarations.comments: Subscribe to an EventSource. Returns an unsubscribe effect.
declarations.title: subscribe

declarations.comments:
Upload scalar for GraphQL file uploads.
At the schema level, Upload is a String scalar (filename).
Actual file data is injected by the multipart HTTP handler.
declarations.title: Upload

declarations.comments: Run all validation rules, returning GraphQL errors
declarations.title: validate

declarations.comments: Validate that query depth does not exceed a maximum
declarations.title: validateDepth

declarations.comments: Validate total number of fields does not exceed a maximum
declarations.title: validateFieldCount

declarations.comments: Validate query complexity with a simple cost model (each field = 1, each list multiplier = 10)
declarations.title: validateComplexity

declarations.comments: Metrics computed in a single pass over the selection tree
declarations.title: SelectionMetrics

declarations.comments: Compute depth, field count, and cost in a single traversal
declarations.title: analyzeSelections

declarations.comments: A collection of wrappers that can be applied to a schema
declarations.title: Wrapper

declarations.comments: Overall wrapper: wraps the entire query processing pipeline
declarations.title: OverallWrapper

declarations.comments: Parsing wrapper: wraps query parsing
declarations.title: ParsingWrapper

declarations.comments: Validation wrapper: wraps query validation
declarations.title: ValidationWrapper

declarations.comments: Execution wrapper: wraps query execution
declarations.title: ExecutionWrapper

declarations.comments: Field wrapper: wraps individual field resolution
declarations.title: FieldWrapper

declarations.comments: Introspection wrapper: wraps introspection queries
declarations.title: IntrospectionWrapper

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: reject queries deeper than maxDepth
declarations.title: maxDepth

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: reject queries with more than maxFields total fields
declarations.title: maxFields

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: reject queries exceeding a cost budget
declarations.title: maxCost

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: check depth, field count, and cost in a single pass
declarations.title: validateLimits

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: apply a timeout to execution (in milliseconds)
declarations.title: timeout

declarations.comments: Pre-built wrapper: log fields that take longer than a threshold (in milliseconds)
declarations.title: logSlowFields
